engrams v0.2.0

Markdown vs Engrams: The Best of Both Worlds

Many teams document design decisions in README.md files, wikis, or AGENTS.md files. While Markdown is human-readable and version-controlled, it suffers from search limitations and token bloat. Engrams CLI combines the query speed of SQLite with the portability of Markdown.

The Limits of Pure Markdown

When relying solely on static Markdown files for AI context:

  • Token Bloat: To orient an AI agent, you must paste the entire README.md or design docs into the prompt window. As your project grows, this wastes thousands of tokens per turn.
  • Keyword-Only Search: Standard text editors use basic keyword search. If your AI searches for "caching", it will miss files that discuss "Redis performance tuning" unless the word "caching" is explicitly present.
  • No Structural Relationships: Markdown files cannot express relationships programmatically. There is no simple way for an AI to query "which decisions are implemented by this pattern?" or "what progress tasks are blocked by this architecture choice?".

How Engrams CLI Bridges the Gap

Engrams does not replace your Markdown files — it leverages them. It introduces a local SQLite database for fast queries, while using human-readable Markdown exports for team sharing.

Feature Standard Markdown Files Engrams CLI
AI Query Speed Slow (requires parsing entire files) Instant (SQLite FTS5 queries under 5ms)
Token Efficiency Low (must load whole files to prompt) High (queries return only the matching entries)
Relationships Manual links (fragile, hard to parse) Knowledge Graph (explicit link relationships)
Git Versioning Excellent (native text) Excellent (via engrams export Markdown)

The Git-Sync Hybrid Workflow

Engrams CLI implements a hybrid workflow that gives you the advantages of both:

  1. Local SQLite for Agents: During active development, your AI runs fast CLI commands (like engrams decision search) against the local engrams/context.db SQLite file.
  2. Markdown for Git Commits: Before committing, you run engrams export. This dumps all decisions, patterns, and progress into a structured engrams_export/ directory containing clean, versionable Markdown files.
  3. Teammate Pull & Import: Teammates pull your branch, see the updated Markdown files, and run engrams import. This immediately syncs their local SQLite database to match yours.

Summary

Use Markdown files for human developer references (like standard README.md files), and use the Engrams CLI to store structural context (ADRs, patterns, tasks) that your AI assistant needs to retrieve efficiently and act upon.
